2604.24594.title: Skill Retrieval Augmentation for Agentic AI
2604.24594.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) evolve into agentic problem solvers, they increasingly rely on external, reusable skills to handle tasks beyond their native parametric capabilities. In existing agent systems, the dominant strategy for incorporating skills is to explicitly enumerate available skills within the context window. However, this strategy fails to scale: as skill corpora expand, context budgets are consumed rapidly, and the agent becomes markedly less accurate in identifying the right skill. To this end, this paper formulates Skill Retrieval Augmentation (SRA), a new paradigm in which agents dynamically retrieve, incorporate, and apply relevant skills from large external skill corpora on demand. To make this problem measurable, we construct a large-scale skill corpus and introduce SRA-Bench, the first benchmark for decomposed evaluation of the full SRA pipeline, covering skill retrieval, skill incorporation, and end-task execution. SRA-Bench contains 5,400 capability-intensive test instances and 636 manually constructed gold skills, which are mixed with web-collected distractor skills to form a large-scale corpus of 26,262 skills. Extensive experiments show that retrieval-based skill augmentation can substantially improve agent performance, validating the promise of the paradigm. At the same time, we uncover a fundamental gap in skill incorporation: current LLM agents tend to load skills at similar rates, regardless of whether a gold skill is retrieved or whether the task actually requires external capabilities. This shows that the bottleneck in skill augmentation lies not only in retrieval but also in the base model's ability to determine which skill to load and when external loading is actually needed. These findings position SRA as a distinct research problem and establish a foundation for the scalable augmentation of capabilities in future agent systems.
2604.24594.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）演變為具有代理性問題解決能力的系統，它們越來越依賴外部的可重用技能來處理超出其原生參數能力的任務。在現有的代理系統中，納入技能的主要策略是在上下文窗口中明確列出可用的技能。然而，這一策略無法擴展：隨著技能語料庫的擴大，上下文預算迅速被消耗，代理在識別正確技能方面的準確性顯著下降。為此，本文提出了技能檢索增強（SRA）的概念，這是一種新的範式，代理可以根據需求動態檢索、納入和應用來自大型外部技能語料庫的相關技能。為了使這個問題可測量，我們構建了一個大規模的技能語料庫，並引入了SRA-Bench，這是第一個針對完整SRA流程的分解評估基準，涵蓋技能檢索、技能納入和最終任務執行。SRA-Bench包含5,400個需要高能力的測試實例和636個手動構建的金標技能，這些技能與網絡收集的干擾技能混合，形成了一個包含26,262個技能的大規模語料庫。大量實驗表明，基於檢索的技能增強可以顯著提高代理性能，驗證了這一範式的潛力。同時，我們發現技能納入存在根本性差距：當前的LLM代理往往以相似的速度加載技能，無論是檢索到金標技能還是任務實際上是否需要外部能力。這顯示出技能增強的瓶頸不僅在於檢索，還在於基礎模型判斷何時加載技能以及何時實際需要外部加載的能力。這些發現將SRA定位為一個獨特的研究問題，並為未來代理系統能力的可擴展增強奠定了基礎。
2604.24594.author: Weihang Su et.al.
2604.24594.authors: Weihang Su, Jianming Long, Qingyao Ai, Yichen Tang, Changyue Wang, Yiteng Tu, Yiqun Liu
